info.title: agentsfleet Control Plane API
info.description: Fleet automation control plane — webhook-driven fleet execution in a bwrap+landlock sandbox.

tags.name: Health
tags.description: System status and readiness probes

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: CLI login flow via device-code style sessions and GitHub OAuth

tags.name: Workspaces
tags.description: Create and manage workspaces (one workspace per repo)

tags.name: Tenant
tags.description: Tenant-scoped surfaces — provider posture and tenant-level configuration

tags.name: auth-identity-events
tags.description: Internal auth-plane identity-lifecycle events (Clerk `user.created`, future identity-provider hooks). Not for customer fleets.

tags.name: Fleets
tags.description: Fleet management and webhook ingestion — create, configure, and operate event-driven fleets

tags.name: Memory
tags.description: Persistent Fleet memory — list and search key-value entries scoped to a Fleet (read-only; entries are written by the runner plane, not this API)

tags.name: Secrets
tags.description: Workspace secret vault — self-managed (Bring Your Own Key) LLM provider keys and arbitrary named secrets a Fleet interpolates as `${secrets.<name>.<field>}`

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Platform-wide configuration — manage shared LLM provider keys

tags.name: Billing
tags.description: Plan upgrades and billing events

tags.name: Integration Grants
tags.description: Fleet runtime permission grants for third-party services (Slack, GitHub, etc.)

tags.name: Connectors
tags.description: Workspace connectors — auth + credential plumbing for third-party providers. One generic `{provider}` route set serves every provider in the deployment's connector registry; unknown providers are 404.

tags.name: Fleet Keys
tags.description: Per-Fleet API keys for LangGraph/CrewAI/Composio Fleets to call the execute endpoint. Workspace-scoped.

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Tenant-level admin API keys (`agt_t` prefix). Rotatable, self-service, replace the legacy `API_KEY` env var for admin auth.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Approval-gate webhooks invoked by humans to unblock a paused fleet

tags.name: Approvals
tags.description: Approval-gate inbox — list, inspect, and resolve pending gates from the dashboard or programmatically.

tags.name: Fleet
tags.description: Runner fleet operator plane — enroll, list, and administer sandbox runners. Operator/admin-scoped.

tags.name: Fleet Bundles
tags.description: Workspace-scoped fleet source bundles imported from templates, uploads, or GitHub.

tags.name: Fleet library
tags.description: Two-tier Fleet library catalog — platform-wide and tenant-scoped onboarding from a GitHub source, stored once in object storage by content hash.

tags.name: Model Catalogue
tags.description: Public model→capability catalogue consumed at provisioning time.

get.description: Returns 200 as long as the process is alive and able to serve HTTP. Does not probe dependencies — use /readyz for readiness (database + queue).

commit.description: Git commit SHA of the running build

get.description: Returns Prometheus-format metrics for scraping by monitoring infrastructure.
Kubernetes-style readiness probe. Returns 200 when both the database and the Redis queue are reachable; returns 503 fail-closed if either dependency is degraded.

503.description: At least one dependency is degraded; ready=false and the failing dependency's boolean is false

get.description: Lists all configured platform-wide LLM provider keys. These are shared keys that workspaces fall back to when no self-managed key is configured.

put.description: Configures a platform-wide LLM provider key by referencing a workspace that has a self-managed key stored. The platform key proxies through the referenced workspace's credential.

source_workspace_id.description: Workspace whose self-managed key to use as the platform key

delete.description: Removes a platform-wide LLM provider key. Workspaces without self-managed keys will no longer be able to use this provider.

post.description:
Step 1 of the command-line device flow. The command-line tool generates
an ephemeral P-256 ECDH keypair and POSTs the public key plus a
human-facing `token_name` label. The server allocates a UUIDv7
`session_id`, builds the browser approval `login_url` from `APP_URL`,
and stores `{cli_public_key, token_name, status: pending, expires_at_ms}`
in Redis with a 5-minute TTL. The command-line tool prints `login_url`
for the human to open in their browser.

Unauthenticated; rate-limited at the Cloudflare WAF edge
(L2 — 10 / IP / minute on `POST /v1/auth/sessions`) and
Clerk-edge (L1) for sign-in / sign-up traffic upstream.

public_key.description:
Base64url-encoded P-256 SubjectPublicKeyInfo. The CLI's
ephemeral ECDH public key — used by the dashboard to
derive the AES-256-GCM session key that wraps the JWT.

token_name.description:
Operator-facing label for the resulting credential
(e.g. `macos-cli`, `linux-cli`, `windows-cli`).
Printable ASCII; appears in audit events and
`agentsfleet auth status`.

session_id.description: UUIDv7 session identifier for approval and verification endpoints.

login_url.description: Browser approval URL for this login session.

get.description:
Step 2 of the CLI device flow. The CLI polls this endpoint with the
`session_id` returned from `POST /v1/auth/sessions`. The response
surfaces only the public material the CLI needs to proceed:
`status`, `cli_public_key`, `token_name`, `expires_at_ms`.

**NEVER** returns `ciphertext`, `nonce`, `dashboard_public_key`,
`verification_code`, or `verification_code_hmac` (Invariant 1 in
`docs/AUTH.md` Flow 1). Ciphertext release is gated behind the
verification code on `POST /v1/auth/sessions/{id}/verify`.

Unauthenticated by design — the CLI poll precedes any token grant.
The terminal states (`expired`, `consumed`, `aborted`) return 410
with a structured ErrorBody (`UZ-AUTH-006/012/013`).

200.description: Session is in an active (non-terminal) state

status.description:
`pending` — created, awaiting dashboard approval.
`verification_pending` — dashboard approved, awaiting CLI
verification code.

cli_public_key.description: Echo of the CLI's ECDH public key (base64url P-256 SPKI).

token_name.description: Operator label that the dashboard shows to the user during approval.

expires_at_ms.description: Unix epoch milliseconds. Session TTL is 5 minutes from creation.

401.description: Session expired (`UZ-AUTH-006`) — the 5-minute TTL elapsed before this poll.

delete.summary: Explicit cancel of a single login session
delete.description:
Used by the dashboard's "cancel this login" button and by the future
sessions surface. Transitions the named session to `aborted` with
`reason="explicit_cancel"`. The Clerk JWT MUST match the session's
`clerk_user_id` (set on `PATCH /v1/auth/sessions/{id}/approve`);
otherwise 403.

patch.summary: Dashboard approves a pending session and submits the encrypted JWT
patch.description:
Step 3 of the CLI device flow. Called by the dashboard JS process
after the operator clicks Approve on `/cli-auth/{session_id}`.

The dashboard:
  1. Generates an ephemeral P-256 ECDH keypair (`dash_priv` / `dash_pub`).
  2. Derives the shared secret = `dash_priv × cli_public_key`.
  3. Runs HKDF-SHA-256 with empty salt and `info = "m74-002-v1"` to
     produce a 32-byte AES-256-GCM key.
  4. Encrypts the Clerk JWT (template `api`) with a fresh 96-bit nonce
     via `crypto.subtle.encrypt({ name: "AES-GCM", iv: nonce }, key, jwt)`.
  5. Generates a 6-digit verification code via
     `crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint32Array(1)) mod 1_000_000`.
  6. PATCHes this endpoint with `{ dashboard_public_key, ciphertext,
     nonce, verification_code }`.

The API computes `verification_code_hmac =
HMAC-SHA256(AUTH_SESSION_CODE_PEPPER, session_id || verification_code)`
and persists the HMAC (never the plaintext code). The session
transitions atomically `pending → verification_pending`. The
single-write invariant: any subsequent PATCH for the same session
returns 409 `UZ-AUTH-015`.

Memory hygiene on the dashboard: discard `dash_priv` and the shared
key after this call resolves. Never persist crypto material.

dashboard_public_key.description: Base64url P-256 SubjectPublicKeyInfo (the dashboard's ephemeral ECDH public key).

ciphertext.description: Base64url AES-256-GCM output over the Clerk JWT plaintext.

nonce.description: Base64url 12-byte (96-bit) AES-GCM nonce. Random per call.

verification_code.description:
Exactly 6 ASCII digits. Travels plaintext over TLS to this
Clerk-authenticated endpoint, exists momentarily in the API
process to compute the HMAC, then is discarded. The
alternative (dashboard pre-hashes locally) does not work
because only the API holds the pepper.

post.summary: CLI submits the 6-digit verification code and receives the encrypted JWT
post.description:
Step 4 of the CLI device flow. Atomically transitions
`verification_pending → consumed` and releases the ciphertext
payload in a single Lua-EVAL write.

No bearer authentication — the verification code IS the auth. The
code is single-use; success and failure paths both surface through
ErrorBody response codes:

  * `UZ-AUTH-011` (400) — code did not match; `verification_attempts`
    incremented. The fifth failed attempt transitions the session to
    `aborted` with `reason="rate_limit_exceeded"` (the L3 rate limit
    per `docs/AUTH.md` Flow 1).
  * `UZ-AUTH-018` (400) — verification code malformed (must be exactly
    6 ASCII digits). The CLI distinguishes this from `UZ-AUTH-011` to
    reprompt for a correctly-shaped code rather than reporting a mismatch.
  * `UZ-AUTH-012` (410) — session already consumed (outside the 60-second
    same-fingerprint replay window, or different fingerprint).
  * `UZ-AUTH-013` (410) — session aborted (5 wrong codes, explicit cancel,
    or replaced by a newer session).
  * `UZ-AUTH-014` (410) — session not yet approved (no dashboard PATCH yet).
  * `UZ-AUTH-006` (401) — session expired (5-min TTL elapsed).

On success the response carries `{ dashboard_public_key, ciphertext,
nonce }`. The CLI derives the same AES-256-GCM key (ECDH × HKDF as
on the dashboard side; `info` MUST be `"m74-002-v1"` verbatim) and
decrypts the JWT.

Consume-idempotency window: on success the server records
`consumed_client_fingerprint = sha256(remote_addr || user_agent || session_id)`
and `consume_payload_expires_at_ms = now_ms + 60_000`. A second
POST inside that window from the same fingerprint returns the same
payload (so "consume succeeded, response lost, client retried"
converges). Outside the window or from a different fingerprint →
410 `UZ-AUTH-012`.

verification_code.description: The 6-digit code the dashboard displayed to the user.

200.description:
Verification succeeded; ciphertext payload released. The CLI
decrypts `ciphertext` using `dashboard_public_key` + its own
ECDH private key per the HKDF / AES-GCM derivation described
on `PATCH /v1/auth/sessions/{session_id}/approve`.

dashboard_public_key.description: Base64url P-256 SPKI — the dashboard's ephemeral ECDH public key from the matching approve call.

ciphertext.description: Base64url AES-256-GCM output over the Clerk JWT.

nonce.description: Base64url 12-byte AES-GCM nonce used during encryption.

delete.summary: Bulk-abort every in-flight login session for the caller
delete.description:
Caller-scoped bulk delete. Enumerates every session with the caller's
`clerk_user_id` whose status is `pending` or `verification_pending`
and transitions each to `aborted` with `reason="explicit_cancel"`.

Does NOT revoke already-minted JWTs — Clerk revocation is a separate
problem; an active CLI continues to work until its short-lived JWT
expires. This endpoint clears in-flight login sessions only.

200.description: Sessions aborted (count includes 0 when none were pending)

aborted_count.description: Number of in-flight sessions transitioned to aborted

get.summary: List or search memory entries for a fleet
get.description: Returns memory entries for the fleet. Without `query` this lists most-recent entries (optionally filtered by `category`). With `query`, performs case-insensitive LIKE search across both `key` and `content` (LIKE metacharacters in `query` are escaped — a query of `%` does NOT return every entry).

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Fuzzy text search across key and content. When omitted, the endpoint lists most-recent entries.

post.description:
Internal auth-plane endpoint. Clerk delivers `user.created` (and adjacent
identity-lifecycle types) via Svix; the signature is verified inline
against `CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. On success the handler atomically
provisions a personal account (tenant + user + owner membership + default
workspace + zero-cent credit state + audit row). Idempotent on
`oidc_subject` — replays return `created:false` with no new rows.
Non-`user.created` event types are 200-ignored so Clerk stops retrying.

Sits outside `/v1/webhooks/` (customer data plane) — the auth namespace
is for signals about the platform's own auth lifecycle, not for events
that wake user-defined Fleets.

parameters.name: svix-id
parameters.description: Svix message identifier (used for deduplication and signature binding).

parameters.name: svix-timestamp
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of the Svix delivery (used for replay protection).

parameters.name: svix-signature
parameters.description: Space-separated list of Svix v1 signatures.

type.description: Clerk event type. Only `user.created` triggers a bootstrap; other types return 200 ignored.

data.description:
Clerk user payload. The handler reads `id`, `email_addresses`,
`primary_email_address_id`, `first_name`, and `last_name`; unknown fields are tolerated.

200.description: Handled. Shape depends on event type: user.created → {workspace_id, workspace_name, created}; user.deleted → {deleted}; any other type → {status: ignored, type}.

workspace_id.description: UUIDv7 of the newly provisioned default workspace (or the existing one on replay).

created.description: `true` on fresh bootstrap; `false` on idempotent replay.

status.description: `ignored` for event types other than user.created/user.deleted.

type.description: Echoed Clerk event type on ignored events.

post.description: Ingress endpoint for Svix-signed webhooks (e.g. Clerk user lifecycle events). Signature is verified by the webhook auth middleware against the workspace's Svix secret before the handler runs. Body shape is event-specific and defined by the upstream issuer; the server validates the envelope and dispatches to the fleet's event log.

parameters.name: fleet_id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the fleet receiving the webhook.

parameters.name: svix-id
parameters.description: Svix message identifier (used for deduplication and signature binding).

parameters.name: svix-timestamp
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of the Svix delivery (used for replay protection).

parameters.name: svix-signature
parameters.description: Space-separated list of Svix signatures (v1 scheme).

schema.description: Opaque event envelope; upstream issuer (Svix) defines the payload shape per event type.

200.description: Not enqueued, intentionally. Two shapes: duplicate delivery (`{"status": "duplicate"}` — same body `event_id` within the 24-hour dedup window) or event ignored (`{"ignored": "fleet_paused"}` — fleet is paused; the dedup slot is not consumed, so redelivery after resume processes normally).

202.description: Webhook accepted and queued for processing.

post.summary: Deliver an inbound webhook event to a Fleet
post.description: Delivers an inbound event to the fleet identified by `fleet_id`. The fleet is looked up by primary key — no JSONB traversal, no source-name uniqueness required. The body MUST be HMAC-SHA256 signed with the workspace credential keyed by the fleet's `trigger.source` (e.g. vault row `fleet:linear`, field `webhook_secret`). The provider-specific signature header (`x-hub-signature-256` for GitHub, `x-slack-signature` for Slack, etc.) is verified upstream by the `webhook_sig` middleware; this handler runs only after the HMAC is valid. There is no Bearer fallback — every inbound webhook MUST be signed. Duplicate events (same `event_id` within 24 h) return 200 `{status: "duplicate"}`. A webhook for a paused fleet returns 200 `{ignored: "fleet_paused"}` without consuming the dedup slot, so redelivery after resume processes normally. A new event is enqueued to the fleet's Redis stream and returns 202.

parameters.name: fleet_id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the target fleet. Obtained from `POST /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/fleets` or `agentsfleet install --from <path>`.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event. Used for idempotency deduplication (24-hour window).

data.description: Event payload — any JSON object. Passed as-is into the fleet event loop.

200.description: Not enqueued, intentionally. Two shapes: duplicate event (`{"status": "duplicate"}` — already processed within the 24-hour dedup window) or event ignored (`{"ignored": "fleet_paused"}` — fleet is paused; the dedup slot is not consumed, so redelivery after resume processes normally).

202.description: Event accepted and enqueued for the fleet.

401.description: Webhook auth failure. Three distinct codes: `UZ-WH-020 webhook_credential_not_configured` (fleet has no recognized provider, OR the workspace has no matching `fleet:<source>` vault credential, OR the credential JSON has no `webhook_secret` field — this is the operator-recoverable misconfig path); `UZ-WH-010 invalid_signature` (provider + secret are configured but the request is unsigned, mis-signed, or the body was tampered with); `UZ-WH-011 stale_timestamp` (Slack-style schemes with timestamp drift > 5 min).

404.description: No active fleet is configured for this source (UZ-WH-001).

post.description: Called by a human (via email link or Slack action) to approve or reject a paused fleet. Body is HMAC-signed by the issuer; the signature is verified against the fleet's webhook secret.

action_id.description: The approval gate's action_id to resolve.

post.summary: Approve or reject an integration grant request
post.description: Human-in-the-loop approval webhook for integration grant requests (Slack, GitHub, etc.). HMAC-signed.

nonce.description: Single-use anti-replay nonce issued with the approval request.

post.description: GitHub `workflow_run` webhook receiver. Body is HMAC-SHA256 signed (X-Hub-Signature-256) against the workspace's `fleet:github` credential `webhook_secret` field. Only `action=completed` with `conclusion=failure` is XADDed to the fleet event stream; other events return 200. Idempotency keyed on `X-GitHub-Delivery` for 24h.

parameters.name: fleet_id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the fleet that owns this webhook receiver.

parameters.name: X-GitHub-Event
parameters.description: GitHub event type. Only `workflow_run` is ingested.

parameters.name: X-GitHub-Delivery
parameters.description: GitHub-issued delivery UUID. Used as the dedupe key.

parameters.name: X-Hub-Signature-256
parameters.description: HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body, prefixed with `sha256=`.

200.description: Duplicate delivery (idempotency hit) or event ignored (fleet paused, non-workflow_run, non-completed action, or non-failure conclusion). Body distinguishes the two via the `deduped` or `ignored` field. The `fleet_paused` ignore does not consume the dedup slot, so redelivery after resume processes normally.

ignored.description: Reason the event was filtered out (e.g. `non_failure_conclusion`, `fleet_paused`).

get.summary: List the connector catalog for a workspace
get.description: Renders the deployment's connector registry as data for the dashboard (requires the `connector:read` scope) — every provider with its archetype, display name, whether the platform side is `configured` (the `<provider>-app` platform bag exists), and whether this workspace is `connected` (holds a `fleet:<provider>` vault handle). The dashboard renders its cards from this — there is no hard-coded provider list. This is the collection whose items are the per-provider status routes below.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the workspace to report connected state for.

200.description: The connector catalog — one entry per registry provider.

configured.description: Platform side ready — the `<provider>-app` bag exists.

connected.description: This workspace holds a `fleet:<provider>` vault handle.

post.description: Begins the connect round-trip for any provider in the connector registry (requires the `connector:write` scope). Returns the URL the operator's browser is redirected to — the provider's OAuth authorize URL (oauth2 archetype, e.g. `slack`) or the vendor App install URL (app_install archetype, e.g. `github`). A single-use signed state bound to the workspace rides that URL; the round-trip finishes at the callback. No token is created or stored here. Unknown provider → 404 `UZ-CONN-004`; provider not provisioned on this deployment → 503 `UZ-CONN-001`.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the workspace the connector will be bound to.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Connector registry id (`slack`, `github`, `zoho`, `jira`, `linear`). The id is also the `{provider}` segment of the callback URL and the vault-key stem.

install_url.description: URL to redirect the operator's browser to (provider authorize URL or vendor install URL).

get.description: Reports whether the workspace holds a live `fleet:{provider}` vault handle (requires the `connector:read` scope). Never fabricates a connected state — a missing or unreadable handle reads as `not_connected`. Provider-specific extras ride alongside (`slack` adds `team`, generic OAuth providers add `label`). Unknown provider → 404 `UZ-CONN-004`.

team.description: Slack only — the connected Slack workspace name (absent for other providers).

label.description: Generic OAuth providers only — display label from the provider handle, when available.

get.summary: Complete a connector connect flow (vendor redirect)
get.description: The provider redirects the operator's browser here after authorization. Bearer-less by design — the signed single-use `state` minted at connect is the only trust anchor (there is no Bearer on a cross-site redirect); verify-and-consume yields the bound workspace. oauth2 providers exchange the `code` for a token under an enforced vendor deadline (`UZ-CONN-003` on stall) and vault it; app_install providers (`github`) record the `installation_id` handle instead. On success the browser is 302-redirected to the dashboard's connector card; if that redirect URL cannot be built (dashboard base URL allocation failure) the connection still succeeded, so it returns 200 `{"status":"connected"}` instead. Invalid, expired, or replayed state gives 400 `UZ-CONN-002`.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: The signed single-use state minted at connect. Binds the callback to a workspace; expired or replayed states are rejected.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: OAuth authorization code (oauth2 providers — required for them).

parameters.name: installation_id
parameters.description: Vendor installation id (app_install providers — required for them; `github` sends it after the App install).

200.description: Connected, but the dashboard redirect URL could not be built (allocation failure). The connection succeeded; no redirect is issued.

302.description: Connected — browser redirected to the dashboard's connector card.

Location.description: Dashboard connectors page for the just-connected provider.

post.description: Slack's Events API receiver (bespoke — inbound event surfaces are per-provider by nature). Bearer-less; every request is authenticated by the Slack v0 request signature (`X-Slack-Signature` + timestamp, 5-minute drift window) — signature failures are 401 `UZ-SLK-010`/`UZ-SLK-011`. `url_verification` echoes the challenge; event deliveries are acknowledged 200 within Slack's deadline and processed async (duplicates dedup to `{"status":"duplicate"}`; events from teams with no connector install are dropped with `{"ignored":"UZ-SLK-020"}` so Slack stops retrying).

parameters.name: X-Slack-Request-Timestamp
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of the delivery; rejected outside the 5-minute drift window.

schema.description: Slack Events API envelope (`url_verification` or `event_callback`) — see https://api.slack.com/apis/events-api#events-JSON.

200.description: Acknowledged. Shapes: `{"status":"accepted"}` (enqueued), `{"status":"duplicate"}` (dedup hit), `{"challenge":"..."}` (url_verification echo), `{"ignored":"UZ-SLK-020"}` (no install for the sending team).

post.description: Creates a new workspace in the caller's tenant. All fields are optional — an empty body provisions a workspace with a server-assigned Heroku-style name.

name.description: Human-readable workspace name. Must be unique within the tenant. If omitted, the server assigns a Heroku-style name (e.g. `jolly-harbor-482`).
Human-readable workspace name. Always present — caller-supplied or server-generated Heroku-style.

get.description: Returns the tenant-scoped billing row created at signup. The single row carries the credit-pool balance shared across every workspace the tenant owns. Two-rate metering in nanos: events are free (both postures); stages cost $0.001 (platform) or $0.0001 (self-managed). Debited by the worker as fleet runs complete.

balance_nanos.description: Remaining credit-pool balance in nanos (1 nano = 1/1,000,000,000 USD).

is_exhausted.description: True once the balance has hit zero on a worker debit.

exhausted_at.description: Epoch ms of the first exhausting debit. Null until the balance is exhausted.

active.description: True while the free-trial window is in effect.

ends_at_ms.description: Epoch ms when the free-trial window closes.

get.description: Returns the newest-first slice of `fleet_execution_telemetry` rows for the caller's tenant. Two rows per event under the credit-pool model (`charge_type` ∈ `receive`, `stage`); the dashboard groups them by `event_id` to show one line per event with both charges. Limit-only paging is sufficient for the v2.0 Settings → Billing Usage tab; deeper history pages with Stripe billing in v2.1.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination token returned in `next_cursor` from a previous page. Omit for the first page. Treat as a black-box string — the encoded `(recorded_at, id)` tuple is an implementation detail.

200.description: Newest-first telemetry rows scoped to the caller's tenant.

next_cursor.description: Opaque token for the next page; null when no more rows.

get.summary: Tenant metering periods for one charge
get.description: Returns the oldest-first renewal slices behind one `stage` charge. The route is scoped to the caller's tenant through the matching `fleet_execution_telemetry` row; unknown or foreign `event_id` values return an empty `items` list.

200.description: Metering slices for the requested event.

get.summary: List every workspace the caller's tenant owns
get.description: Returns the full set of workspaces owned by the caller's tenant. Backs the dashboard workspace switcher: a user can switch to any workspace in this list without re-issuing the JWT, because workspace authorisation is tenant-scoped. No pagination — a tenant's workspace count is bounded and small in practice.

name.description: Human-readable workspace name. Nullable — pre-rename workspaces may not have one.

get.description: Returns the tenant's persisted provider config — mode, provider name, model, context cap, and (under self-managed) the user-named credential_ref. The api_key is never serialised. Tenants with no row receive a synthesised platform default.

put.description: Eager-validates and UPSERTs the tenant_model_selection row. Validation order: body shape → credential_ref required (mode=self_managed) → credential_not_found → credential_data_malformed → model_not_in_caps_catalogue → UPSERT. Stored `provider` is read from the credential JSON; effective `model` is the body override (when present) else the credential's stored model. A self_managed activation also registers the matching model entry (see /v1/tenants/me/models) when none exists — idempotent on repeat, so an activation is always visible as a registry row.

credential_ref.description: User-named vault key. Required when mode=self_managed.

model.description: Optional model override. Falls back to the credential's stored model when absent.

delete.summary: Reset tenant LLM provider to platform default
delete.description: Equivalent to `PUT {mode: "platform"}`. Writes the explicit mode=platform row so the dashboard can distinguish "never configured" from "explicitly reset".

get.description: One entry per configured model; two entries can share a `secret_ref`. Each entry is joined to its secret's non-secret metadata (provider, kind, base_url, has_key) — `api_key` is never serialised. `active` is computed against the tenant's current provider selection (`GET /v1/tenants/me/provider`). Before listing, a pre-registry activation with no matching entry is synthesized idempotently, so the registry self-heals with no separate backfill.

post.description: Creates one `(model_id, secret_ref)` row. `secret_ref` must already name a vault secret in the tenant's primary workspace — store or reuse one via `/v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/secrets` first. Does not activate the entry; activate via `PUT /v1/tenants/me/provider`.

secret_ref.description: User-named vault secret; may already back other entries.

404.description: secret_ref does not name a vault secret (`UZ-MODELS-002`).

409.description: An entry with this exact (model_id, secret_ref) pair already exists (`UZ-MODELS-003`).

patch.description: Model-only change; `secret_ref` is immutable on this endpoint — create a new entry to point at a different secret.

404.description: No entry with this id for the calling tenant (`UZ-MODELS-004`).

409.description: The new (model_id, secret_ref) pair collides with an existing entry (`UZ-MODELS-003`).

delete.description: Idempotent — deleting an id that doesn't exist (already removed, or never existed) still returns 204. The referenced vault secret is never touched; sibling entries sharing it survive.

409.description: This entry is the tenant's active selection — switch to another entry first (`UZ-MODELS-001`).

get.summary: List secrets stored for a workspace
get.description: Returns secret names and non-secret descriptors. Values are never returned — they live encrypted in `vault.secrets`.

post.summary: Store a secret in the workspace vault
post.description:
Plaintext is the canonical-stringified form of the supplied JSON object,
envelope-encrypted with the KEK (master key v1) and persisted to
`vault.secrets`. The raw value is never stored unencrypted and never
returned. `data` must be a non-empty JSON object whose stringified form
is ≤ 4 KiB; a stringified form over 4 KiB is rejected with `UZ-VAULT-002`,
and bare strings, arrays, scalars, and `{}` are rejected with
`UZ-VAULT-001`.

name.description: Secret identifier a Fleet interpolates as `${secrets.<name>.<field>}`.

data.description:
JSON object whose top-level keys are the field names a skill
references via `${secrets.<name>.<field>}`. The runtime never
inspects the schema — this is opaque to the platform.

patch.description:
Replaces only the stored `api_key` field; `provider`/`model`/`base_url`
(and any other stored fields) are preserved by loading the existing
object and swapping the single field. A missing secret returns
`UZ-VAULT-003`.

api_key.description: The new key. Rejected empty or over the 4 KiB stored-object limit (`UZ-VAULT-002`), and never echoed back or logged.

delete.summary: Delete a secret from the workspace vault
delete.description: Idempotent — returns 204 whether or not the secret existed.

post.description: Creates a new `agt_t`-prefixed tenant admin key. **The raw key is returned once.** Only the SHA-256 hash is persisted — the key cannot be retrieved again. Requires operator (or admin) role.

key_name.description: Unique per tenant. Alphanumeric, hyphens, and underscores only.

key.description: Raw `agt_t` tenant key (70 chars). Shown once — store securely.

409.description: Key name already exists for this tenant (`UZ-APIKEY-005`).

get.description: Returns metadata for every tenant API key visible to the caller. Results are always scoped to the caller's tenant; `key_hash` is never returned.

total.description: Total rows across all pages for this tenant.

patch.description: Partial lifecycle update. Body must be `{"active": false}` — re-activation is not supported; mint a new key instead. Sets `revoked_at` and `updated_at`.

409.description: Key is already revoked (`UZ-APIKEY-006`) or attempted to set `active: true` (`UZ-APIKEY-007`).

delete.summary: Delete a revoked tenant API key
delete.description: Hard-deletes the row from `core.api_keys`. The key must already be revoked (`active = false`); an active key returns `409 UZ-APIKEY-008`.

409.description: Active key must be revoked before deletion (`UZ-APIKEY-008`).

get.summary: List fleet keys in a workspace
get.description: Returns fleet-key metadata only — never returns `key_hash`.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

post.summary: Issue a Fleet key for a Fleet
post.description: Creates a new `agt_a` Fleet key bound to a specific Fleet. **The raw key is returned once.** Only the SHA-256 hash is persisted — the key cannot be retrieved again.

key.description: Raw `agt_a` API key. Shown once — store securely.

delete.description: Revokes the Fleet key immediately. Subsequent requests with the key receive 401.

get.description: Returns fleets the caller has access to in the given workspace. Keyset paginated over `(created_at DESC, id DESC)`. Omit `cursor` for the first page; pass the response `cursor` back verbatim to fetch the next page. `cursor` is `null` when the last page has been reached. Response envelope follows the RAD §8 `items`/`total` convention (`total` is the size of this page, not a global count).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque page cursor from a prior response's `cursor` field, formatted `{created_at_epoch_ms}:{fleet_id}`. Omit for the first page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Defaults to 20. Clamped to a maximum of 100.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

cursor.description: Opaque cursor to fetch the next page. `null` when there are no more pages.

post.description: Creates a new Fleet from direct SKILL.md bytes or from a stored Fleet Bundle snapshot. Optional TRIGGER.md bytes override the bundle trigger; otherwise the stored trigger is used, and if absent the server generates a default API trigger. The server is the single parser of trigger frontmatter — `name` and the persisted `config_json` are derived server-side. The Command-Line Interface (CLI) sends markdown files as raw bytes; no frontmatter parsing happens client-side.

requestBody.description: Install a fleet from exactly one onboarded Fleet library tier (M103 §4): a platform library entry (slug id) or this workspace's tenant library entry (UUIDv7). The server reads SKILL/TRIGGER + content identity from the library row; raw-SKILL paste and the legacy per-workspace bundle_id are not accepted.

platform_library_id.description: Slug id of a platform catalog library entry to install.

tenant_library_id.description: UUIDv7 of one of this workspace's tenant library entries to install.

name.description: Optional operator-supplied fleet name (slug-safe, lowercase/digits/hyphen, max 64 chars). Overrides the SKILL.md-derived name so one library entry can back multiple fleets in a workspace, each with its own name + webhooks. Absent ⇒ the SKILL.md `name:`.
Fleet name parsed from TRIGGER.md frontmatter or the generated default trigger — the canonical identifier the user will see in `agentsfleet status` and CLI output.

webhook_urls.description: Map of each webhook trigger's source → its delivery URL (`{api_url}/v1/webhooks/{fleet_id}/{source}`). Empty object when the fleet declares no webhook triggers.

get.summary: List the platform Fleet library catalog
get.description: Returns the first-party (platform) Fleet library catalog. Rows contain only metadata and requirement hints; they carry no credential values or object-store keys. The workspace gallery union lives at GET /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/fleet-libraries.

post.summary: Onboard a platform Fleet library entry
post.description: Onboards a Fleet library entry into the global platform catalog from a GitHub source reference. Requires the `platform-library:write` scope. The canonical bundle is written to internal object storage keyed by content hash; the response carries metadata only — never an object-store key or support-file content.

201.description: Entry onboarded into the platform catalog.

get.summary: List the workspace Fleet library gallery
get.description: Returns the union of the platform Fleet library catalog and this workspace's own tenant entries — and nothing from another workspace. Each entry carries identity, source, requirements, and support-file summaries; never an object-store key.

post.summary: Onboard a tenant Fleet library entry
post.description: Onboards a Fleet library entry into the caller's workspace catalog from a GitHub source reference. Requires the `library:write` scope plus ownership of the target workspace. Re-onboarding identical bytes converges on one `(workspace_id, content_hash)` row. The response carries metadata only.

201.description: Entry onboarded into the workspace catalog.

patch.summary: Update Fleet config or drive its status FSM
patch.description:
Partial update of a Fleet. Body fields are optional and presence-based:

- `config_json` — replace the fleet's config blob whole; publishes
  `fleet_config_changed`.
- `trigger_markdown` — replace the fleet's stored `TRIGGER.md`; the server
  reparses the frontmatter and overlays the `x_agentsfleet.triggers` half of
  `config_json`. The fleet's `name` is rewritten to the reparsed name.
  Mutually exclusive with `config_json` (both drive the same column).
- `source_markdown` — replace the fleet's stored `SKILL.md`; the server
  reparses the frontmatter and overlays the tools / credentials / network /
  budget halves of `config_json`. The reparsed `name` must equal the
  fleet's current name (renames require a fresh install).
- `status` — drive the lifecycle FSM. Allowed values: `active` (resume from
  stopped or auto-paused), `stopped` (halt the running session, resumable),
  `killed` (terminal mark, irreversible). The `paused` state is platform-only
  (set by the anomaly gate) and rejected here.

All field-bearing branches run inside a single transaction that takes a
row-level `FOR UPDATE` lock on `core.fleets`, applies field-level merge
semantics, and commits with a single `UPDATE`. Two writers patching
different body fields land both halves; two writers patching the same
field collapse to last-write-wins. Per-transaction `lock_timeout=5s`,
`statement_timeout=10s`, `idle_in_transaction_session_timeout=5s` make
pathological waits fail fast as `503` (retryable).

The SQL UPDATE is one statement; one control-stream publish fires when any
dirty surface lands. Returns the new `config_revision` (a strictly
monotonic ms-epoch timestamp).

FSM rules:

- `killed` is terminal — any further PATCH on a killed fleet returns 404
  (the row is a tombstone).
- Stop requires status ∈ {active, paused}; Resume requires status ∈
  {stopped, paused}; Kill is allowed from any non-killed state.
- Disallowed transitions return 409 (e.g. resume on an active fleet).

Status transitions (stop/resume/kill) require operator-minimum role
per RULE BIL — destructive lifecycle actions. Pure config updates stay
at workspace-member.

config_json.description:
The new fleet config as a JSON string. Validated server-side.
Mutually exclusive with `trigger_markdown`.

trigger_markdown.description:
The full TRIGGER.md to persist; the server reparses the
frontmatter and overlays the `x_agentsfleet.triggers` half of
`config_json`. Must be non-empty. Mutually exclusive with
`config_json`.

source_markdown.description:
The full SKILL.md to persist; the server reparses the
frontmatter and overlays the tools / credentials / network /
budget halves of `config_json`. The reparsed `name` must
match the fleet's current name. Must be non-empty.

status.description:
Target FSM state. `active` resumes from stopped/paused; `stopped`
halts the running session (resumable); `killed` is terminal. Note
that `paused` is intentionally not in this enum — it is set only
by the platform's anomaly gate.
Present only when the request body included a `status` field. Echoes the new status.

delete.summary: Hard-delete a fleet and cascade its history
delete.description:
Hard-purge a fleet. Removes the `core.fleets` row and cascades every
per-fleet record across PostgreSQL (PG) schemas (events, telemetry, sessions,
approval gates, integration grants, fleet keys, memory entries) plus
best-effort DELs the Redis event stream.

Two-step lifecycle for safety: kill first (PATCH `{status:"killed"}`),
then delete. Returns 409 if the fleet is not yet `killed`.

Operator-minimum role required (RULE BIL — destructive lifecycle).

get.summary: List integration grants for a fleet
get.description: Returns pending, approved, and revoked grants, newest first.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

delete.description: Immediately revokes a grant. A revoked grant blocks the fleet on its next execute attempt against the affected service.

post.description: Called by a fleet (or on its behalf) to request permission to use a third-party integration (e.g. Slack, Gmail). Creates a pending grant that a human approves via the grant-approval webhook.

200.description: Existing grant returned (idempotent — a pending or approved grant for this service already existed).

post.summary: Post a chat message to a fleet
post.description: Build an EventEnvelope with `actor=steer:<user>` / `event_type=chat` and XADD it onto `fleet:{id}:events`. The Redis stream entry id IS the canonical event_id and is returned to the caller so Server-Sent Events (SSE) consumers can filter frames for the message they just sent. Same single-ingress rule as webhook and continuation events — no separate "queued vs instant" mode.

message.description: Operator message body. Up to 8192 bytes. The server wraps it in a JSON object with a "message" field as the envelope's request payload.

202.description: Event enqueued. The CLI uses event_id to filter SSE frames.

event_id.description: Redis stream entry id (the canonical event_id). Format `<ms>-<seq>`.

409.description: Fleet is paused or otherwise not active (`UZ-AGT-012`). Steering refuses loudly instead of enqueuing work that would strand. The problem-details body carries `current_state` naming the actual fleet status (e.g. `paused`). Resume with `agentsfleet resume <fleet>`, then retry.

get.description: Cursor-paginated list of operator-visible events for one fleet, newest first. Each row is a `core.fleet_events` entry — the durable record produced by the worker write path on every steer, webhook, cron, or continuation event. Filter by actor glob (e.g. `steer:*`, `webhook:github`) or `since=` (Go-style duration like `2h`/`30m` or RFC 3339 timestamp). `since=` and `cursor=` are mutually exclusive.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque base64url-no-pad cursor returned in `next_cursor`. Mutually exclusive with `since`.

parameters.name: actor
parameters.description: Glob filter against the row's actor. `*` is a wildcard. Examples - 'steer:kishore', 'webhook:github', 'webhook:*'.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Lower bound on `created_at`. Accepts Go-style durations (15s/30m/2h/7d) or RFC 3339 timestamps. Mutually exclusive with `cursor`.

get.summary: Subscribe to a fleet's live activity stream (SSE)
get.description: Server-Sent Events tail of the Redis pub/sub channel `fleet:{id}:activity`. The server fans the channel out from one shared subscription and forwards each PUBLISH frame as one SSE event with id (per-connection monotonic counter), event (frame kind), and data (JSON payload) lines. Frame kinds - event_received, tool_call_started, tool_call_progress, chunk, tool_call_completed, event_complete. Sequence ids reset to 0 on every new stream and the server ignores `Last-Event-ID`. Concurrent streams per instance are capped - above the cap the request is rejected with 503 (code UZ-API-002) and a Retry-After header. Clients reconnect by calling GET /events with a cursor to backfill the durable record, then re-open this stream. Authentication this slice is Bearer-only (CLI / programmatic). Browser cookie auth lands with the dashboard slice.

get.summary: List a workspace's aggregate event history
get.description: Cursor-paginated list of operator-visible events across every fleet in the workspace, newest first. Replaces the deleted activity endpoint. Optional `fleet_id` filter narrows to one fleet. Same query shape as the per-fleet endpoint - `actor` glob, `since=` (duration or RFC 3339), and a `cursor` mutually exclusive with `since`.

parameters.name: fleet_id
parameters.description: Drill down to one fleet within the workspace.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a prior response. Mutually exclusive with `since`.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Lower bound on `created_at`. Go-style duration or RFC 3339 timestamp.

get.summary: List pending approval gates for a workspace
get.description:
Returns approval gates oldest-first (oldest is most urgent). Each row
surfaces the fleet's proposed action, gathered evidence, blast-radius
assessment, and timeout countdown. Filter by fleet, gate kind, or
status. Cursor pagination over (requested_at, id) so concurrent
inserts don't cause silent skips.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Defaults to "pending". Supply "approved", "denied", "timed_out" to query terminal states.

get.summary: Get a single approval gate by id
get.description: Drives the dashboard detail page. 404 when the gate doesn't exist or belongs to a different workspace.

post.description:
Resolves the gate to "approved" and wakes the worker thread blocked
on the gate. Funnels through the channel-agnostic resolve core that
Slack callbacks and the auto-timeout sweeper share — first writer
wins; concurrent resolvers receive 409 with the original outcome and
resolver attribution.

Resolves the gate to "denied" and wakes the worker thread. The
worker treats `denied` as "do not execute the proposed tool call".
Same dedup semantics as `:approve`.

Enrolls a runner into the fleet. Authed by an existing operator credential (Clerk JWT or `agt_t` API key with admin role) — there is no enrollment token. Mints a durable `agt_r` runner token (returned once) and stores only its SHA-256 hash.

201.description: Runner registered; the bearer token is returned exactly once.

get.description: Platform-admin operator-plane read of the whole fleet. Paginated, read-only. Each row carries a derived `liveness` — never the stored auth state, never the token hash.

patch.description: Platform-admin operator action on a single runner. `cordon` → cordoned, `drain` → draining, `revoke` → revoked (terminal; a revoked runner cannot transition back).

get.description: Platform-admin read of a single runner's append-only history. Paginated; optionally filtered by event type and an occurred-at millisecond window.

parameters.name: event_type
parameters.description: Filter to a single runner event type.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Lower bound on occurred_at, epoch milliseconds (inclusive).

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Upper bound on occurred_at, epoch milliseconds (inclusive). Must be >= since.

get.description:
Public, unauthenticated model->capability catalogue plus the global, non-secret
client config (run/event rates, starter credit, free-trial window). Both the
install skill and `agentsfleet provider set` call this once at provisioning time
and pin the result.

The path-key segment (`da5b6b3810543fe108d816ee972e4ff8`) is an opportunistic-crawler
deflector, not a credential — the catalogue is public information. Rotation is a
coordinated CLI + skill + API release.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Filter the catalogue to a single `model_id`. Omit to return the whole catalogue.

200.description: The model catalogue, per-token rates, and global billing config.

version.description: Catalogue version, derived as the max model updated_at rendered YYYY-MM-DD (UTC).

id.description: The model_id. Provider hosting is encoded in the id itself.

BearerAuth.description: Obtain a token via the CLI auth flow (POST /v1/auth/sessions) or GitHub OAuth

docs_uri.description: Stable link to documentation for this error code

title.description: Short human-readable label, same for every occurrence of this code

detail.description: Instance-specific context describing what went wrong
detail.example: No fleet with id 'abc123' in this workspace.

current_state.description: Present only on 409 conflict responses (RFC 7807 extension). Names the resource state that forbade the requested transition, e.g. `paused` when steering a paused fleet (UZ-AGT-012).

user_message.description:
Present only for error codes with a curated dashboard-safe rendering
(RFC 7807 extension). One plain sentence with no snake_case tokens,
internal nouns, or HTTP jargon — safe to render directly to an
end user, unlike `detail`/`title` which are written for the
CLI/API-integrator audience and may reference internal state.
Omitted (not `null`) for every code without a curated override;
clients should fall back to `detail`/`title` in that case.
user_message.example: We couldn't find that Fleet. It may have been deleted, or the ID doesn't match one in this workspace.

ReadyzBody.description: Response body for GET /readyz. Identical shape for both 200 (healthy) and 503 (degraded) responses — only the boolean values differ.

ready.description: Overall readiness — true only when every dependency is healthy. Mirrors the HTTP status (true ↔ 200, false ↔ 503).

database.description: True if the PostgreSQL connection pool is reachable and a trivial query succeeds

queue.description: True if the Redis queue backend is reachable

PlatformKey.description: A platform-wide LLM provider key configuration

source_workspace_id.description: Workspace whose self-managed key is used

model.description: The priced (provider, model_id) this default resolves to. Non-null for the active row; NULL for inactive rows.

active.description: Whether this platform key is active

updated_at.description: Epoch milliseconds of the last update.

has_key.description: Whether the referenced secret's body carries a non-empty api_key.

context_cap_tokens.description: From the model-caps catalogue when the provider/model is known; omitted otherwise.

active.description: True when (secret_ref, model_id) matches the tenant's current provider selection.

platform_default_available.description: Whether an active core.platform_provider_defaults row exists, independent of this tenant's own mode.

SecretSummary.description:
A workspace secret's non-secret descriptors — the `api_key`/`data`
payload is never projected here.

kind.description:
Server-derived classification from the stored body's `provider`
field — the client reads this, it never re-guesses from the name.
`custom_secret` also covers legacy/corrupt bodies that fail to
decrypt or parse, so listing always returns 200.

TenantApiKey.description: Metadata for a tenant API key. `key_hash` and the raw key are never returned.

last_used_at.description: Stays null until the async-stamping workstream ships.

FleetTrigger.description: A single trigger projected into the fleet list row.

triggers.description: Projected from config_json `x-agentsfleet.triggers`; null when the row has no triggers entry.

events_processed.description: Lifetime count of events this fleet has processed (rows in `core.fleet_events`).

budget_used_nanos.description: Cumulative credit this fleet has consumed, in nanos (1 nano = 1/1,000,000,000 USD). Sum of its `fleet_execution_telemetry.credit_deducted_nanos`.

required_credentials_reasons.description: Per-credential display-only "why this fleet needs it" copy, keyed by credential name (e.g. {"github": "review your pull requests"}). An install-gate hint, NOT a security control — credential validation reads required_credentials. Optional: older catalogs may omit it.

source_kind.description: Where the entry comes from. `github` and `template` are fetched and validated server-side; `upload` carries the SKILL.md (and optional TRIGGER.md) Markdown inline for a skill-only entry.

source_ref.description: Required for `github` (an `owner/repo` locator resolved at the default branch) and `template` (a first-party template id).

skill_markdown.description: Raw SKILL.md bytes. Required for `upload`; ignored for fetched sources.

trigger_markdown.description: Optional raw TRIGGER.md bytes for `upload`. Ignored for fetched sources.

FleetLibraryOnboardResult.description: Result of onboarding a Fleet library entry (platform or tenant tier). Carries the entry identity, its tier in `visibility`, the content hash, the derived requirements, and support-file summaries — never an object-store key or support-file content.

id.description: Entry id — the slug for platform entries, a UUIDv7 for tenant entries.

visibility.description: The catalog tier the entry was onboarded into.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 content hash of the onboarded snapshot.

FleetLibraryGalleryEntry.description: One gallery row — a platform or tenant Fleet library entry. Carries identity, description, source, derived requirements, per-credential reason copy, and support-file summaries; never an object-store key or support-file content.

description.description: The entry's SKILL.md description, rendered on the gallery card. Platform rows carry their curated copy; tenant rows carry the description derived from the onboarded SKILL frontmatter.

visibility.description: The catalog tier this template belongs to.

required_credentials_reasons.description: Display-only {credential_name: reason} map driving the install gate's purpose copy. Platform rows surface their curated reasons; tenant rows surface an empty object (the importer derives no per-credential copy).

gate_kind.description: Classification driving UI grouping. Empty string when not classified.

proposed_action.description: Human-readable summary of what the fleet wants to do.

evidence.description: Fleet-gathered context (free-form). Rendered as expandable JSON tree.

blast_radius.description: Fleet's assessment of the action's reach.

detail.description: Optional reason text supplied by the resolver.

timeout_at.description: ms epoch — sweeper auto-denies after this point

updated_at.description: Set on terminal transition; doubles as resolved_at.

reason.description: Optional free-form note attached to the resolution.

ApprovalAlreadyResolvedResponse.description:
RFC 7807 problem+json with structured extension fields carrying the
original outcome and resolver attribution so the dashboard can render
"already resolved by X at Y" without a follow-up GET.

RunnerRegisterRequest.description: Self-reported runner identity at enrollment. `sandbox_tier` is telemetry only — placement keys off operator-assigned trust, not this claim.

host_id.description: Operator-supplied host identifier for this runner (1-256 chars).

sandbox_tier.description: Isolation strength the runner self-reports. Stored as telemetry.

RunnerRegisterResponse.description: The durable runner identity plus its bearer token. The `runner_token` is returned exactly once; the server stores only its SHA-256 hash.

runner_token.description: The `agt_r`-prefixed bearer token the runner presents on every later call. Returned once; never retrievable again.

RunnerSummary.description: One fleet row as returned to the operator. `liveness` is derived at read time (never stored); the auth `token_hash` and stored auth state are never returned.

admin_state.description: Operator intent. `active` admits the runner plane; the others reject it.

liveness.description: Derived runtime liveness. `registered` = minted, never seen; `busy` = holds a live lease; `online` = heartbeat fresh; `offline` = heartbeat stale.

last_seen_at.description: Epoch milliseconds of the last heartbeat; 0 if never seen.

created_at.description: Epoch milliseconds the runner was registered.

metadata.description: Event-type-specific metadata (e.g. host_id, from/to admin_state). Shape varies by event_type.
